Overview Request flow HTTP API Storage Roadmap

HTTP API

Five endpoints. Types live in internal/wire, used by both the daemon and the CLI's HTTP client.

Authentication

All /api/* endpoints require a bearer token:

Authorization: Bearer <token from ~/.config/crate/config.yaml>

The token is generated on first run by the daemon (32 random bytes, hex-encoded) and stored with 0600 permissions. The server compares with subtle.ConstantTimeCompare — cheap insurance against timing oracles even though this is localhost-only today.

The public static paths (/, /<site>/...) require no auth — that's the point.

Endpoints

GET /api/status

Unauthenticated. Returns daemon version and site count. Useful as a health check.

{
  "version": "0.1.0-dev",
  "site_count": 5
}

GET /api/sites

Lists deployed sites with metadata.

{
  "sites": [
    {
      "name": "welcome",
      "updated_at": "2026-06-20T13:38:00Z",
      "size_bytes": 1735,
      "file_count": 1
    }
  ]
}

PUT /api/sites/{name}

Replaces the site at {name} with the contents of the request body. The body is a raw tar stream — no JSON wrapper, no multipart.

FieldConstraint
namematches ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9._-]{0,62}$
bodyapplication/x-tar
tar entriesregular files and directories only; symlinks/hardlinks silently skipped; paths starting with .. or / are rejected

Response:

{
  "site": { "name": "foo", "size_bytes": 1234, "file_count": 3, "updated_at": "..." },
  "url":  "http://localhost:7777/foo/"
}

DELETE /api/sites/{name}

Removes the site directory. Returns 204 No Content on success, 404 if the site doesn't exist.

Public static paths

Anything outside /api/* is treated as static content. The first path segment selects the site; everything after is the file path within that site.

Error responses

Non-2xx responses always carry a JSON body:

{ "error": "human-readable message" }

The CLI surfaces the message as crated: 401 Unauthorized: invalid token.
